This is an R Markdown Notebook. When you execute code within the notebook, the results appear beneath the code.

Try executing this chunk by clicking the Run button within the chunk or by placing your cursor inside it and pressing Ctrl+Shift+Enter.

Add a new chunk by clicking the Insert Chunk button on the toolbar or by pressing Ctrl+Alt+I.

When you save the notebook, an HTML file containing the code and output will be saved alongside it (click the Preview button or press Ctrl+Shift+K to preview the HTML file).

The preview shows you a rendered HTML copy of the contents of the editor. Consequently, unlike Knit, Preview does not run any R code chunks. Instead, the output of the chunk when it was last run in the editor is displayed.

rm(list = ls())
setwd("D:/sem8/biostatistics/transcriptome-data-analysis")
getwd()
[1] "D:/sem8/biostatistics/transcriptome-data-analysis"
D:/sem8/biostatistics/transcriptome-data-analysis
infect <- read.table(
  file = "infect.txt",
  header = FALSE,
  sep = "\t",
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

colnames(infect) <- c("SRR", "Infectivity", "CellLine")
infect$Infectivity <- as.numeric(infect$Infectivity)

############################################
## CONDITIONAL COLORING (FIXED)
############################################

infect$color <- ifelse(
  infect$Infectivity < 4, "red3",
  ifelse(infect$Infectivity < 30, "seagreen3", "steelblue")
)

############################################
## SORT
############################################

infect_sorted <- infect[order(infect$Infectivity), ]

############################################
## PLOT
############################################

par(mar = c(4, 10, 2, 1))

barplot(
  infect_sorted$Infectivity,
  horiz = TRUE,
  names.arg = infect_sorted$CellLine,
  las = 1,
  col = infect_sorted$color,
  border = NA,
  xlab = "Nef+/Nef- infectivity ratio",
  xlim = c(0, max(infect_sorted$Infectivity) * 1.1),
  cex.names = 0.8
)

gene_id <- "ENSG00000164300"
expr_folder <- "D:/sem8/biostatistics/transcriptome-data-analysis/gene data"

############################################
## -------- PREP INFECT (NO READING) --------
############################################

# Just ensure columns are correct
colnames(infect) <- c("SRR", "Infectivity", "CellLine")
infect$Infectivity <- as.numeric(infect$Infectivity)

############################################
## -------- STEP 1: MAP SRR IDs --------
############################################

file_to_cellline <- c(
  "SRR2166626" = "HT1080",
  "SRR2166634" = "CEM X 174",
  "SRR2166632" = "IMR90",
  "SRR2166624" = "MT4",
  "SRR2166629" = "DAUDI",
  "SRR2166635" = "WI38",
  "SRR2166630" = "C8166",
  "SRR2166627" = "RAJI",
  "SRR2166625" = "HSB2",
  "SRR2166628" = "CEM SS",
  "SRR2166633" = "CEM A 301",
  "SRR2166631" = "RAMOS",
  "SRR2166637" = "bl41",
  "SRR2166636" = "JURKAT E6.1",
  "SRR2166638" = "Jurkat tag"
)

############################################
## -------- STEP 2: READ EXPRESSION --------
############################################

files <- list.files(expr_folder, full.names = TRUE)

read_expr <- function(file) {
  df <- read.table(file, header = TRUE, sep = "\t", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  df <- df[, 1:2]

  srr <- tools::file_path_sans_ext(basename(file))
  cellline <- file_to_cellline[srr]

  colnames(df) <- c("GeneID", cellline)
  df
}

expr_list <- lapply(files, read_expr)

expr_merged <- Reduce(
  function(x, y) merge(x, y, by = "GeneID", all = TRUE),
  expr_list
)

############################################
## -------- STEP 3: EXTRACT SERINC5 --------
############################################

serinc5 <- expr_merged[expr_merged$GeneID == gene_id, -1]

serinc5 <- as.data.frame(t(serinc5))
serinc5$CellLine <- rownames(serinc5)
rownames(serinc5) <- NULL

colnames(serinc5) <- c("Expression", "CellLine")
serinc5$Expression <- as.numeric(serinc5$Expression)

############################################
## -------- STEP 4: MERGE --------
############################################

data <- merge(infect, serinc5, by = "CellLine")
data <- data[complete.cases(data[, c("Infectivity", "Expression")]), ]

cat("Number of matched samples:", nrow(data), "\n")
Number of matched samples: 15 
############################################
## -------- STEP 5: CONDITIONAL COLORS --------
############################################
## (using your new logic instead of manual mapping)

data$color <- ifelse(
  data$Infectivity < 4, "red3",
  ifelse(data$Infectivity < 30, "seagreen3", "steelblue")
)

############################################
## -------- STEP 6: CORRELATION --------
############################################

ct <- cor.test(data$Expression, data$Infectivity)

r  <- round(ct$estimate, 3)
r2 <- round(r^2, 4)

print(ct)

    Pearson's product-moment correlation

data:  data$Expression and data$Infectivity
t = 7.0696, df = 13, p-value = 8.422e-06
alternative hypothesis: true correlation is not equal to 0
95 percent confidence interval:
 0.6963381 0.9634395
sample estimates:
      cor 
0.8908334 
############################################
## -------- STEP 7: SCATTER PLOT (UPDATED) --------
############################################

# Scale expression
data$Expression_scaled <- data$Expression / 100

par(mar = c(5, 5, 2, 1))

plot(
  data$Infectivity,          # X = infectivity
  data$Expression_scaled,    # Y = scaled expression
  pch = 21,
  bg  = data$color,
  col = "black",
  cex = 1.7,
  xlab = expression("Nef"^"+"*"/Nef"^"-"*" infectivity ratio"),
  ylab = "SERINC5 expression (scaled RPM)"
)

# Regression (IMPORTANT: update model)
abline(lm(Expression_scaled ~ Infectivity, data = data), lwd = 2)

# Annotation
text(
  x = min(data$Infectivity),
  y = max(data$Expression_scaled),
  labels = bquote(
    atop(
      italic(r) == .(r),
      atop(
        italic(R)^2 == .(r2),
        italic(P) < 0.001
      )
    )
  ),
  adj = c(0, 1),
  cex = 1
)

Tests on the line plot.

###Pearson correlation
cor.test(data$Expression, data$Infectivity, method = "pearson")

    Pearson's product-moment correlation

data:  data$Expression and data$Infectivity
t = 7.0696, df = 13, p-value = 8.422e-06
alternative hypothesis: true correlation is not equal to 0
95 percent confidence interval:
 0.6963381 0.9634395
sample estimates:
      cor 
0.8908334 
### linear regression
model <- lm(Expression ~ Infectivity, data = data)
summary(model)

Call:
lm(formula = Expression ~ Infectivity, data = data)

Residuals:
     Min       1Q   Median       3Q      Max 
-2172.36  -384.10    83.13   430.24  2108.28 

Coefficients:
            Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
(Intercept)  -241.97     363.06  -0.666    0.517    
Infectivity   177.61      25.12   7.070 8.42e-06 ***
---
Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

Residual standard error: 1058 on 13 degrees of freedom
Multiple R-squared:  0.7936,    Adjusted R-squared:  0.7777 
F-statistic: 49.98 on 1 and 13 DF,  p-value: 8.422e-06
###Group Comparison (t-test)
data$Group <- ifelse(data$Infectivity < 4, "Low",
               ifelse(data$Infectivity < 30, "Medium", "High"))

t.test(Expression ~ Group, data = subset(data, Group %in% c("Low","High")))

    Welch Two Sample t-test

data:  Expression by Group
t = 5.9146, df = 1.0162, p-value = 0.1039
alternative hypothesis: true difference in means between group High and group Low is not equal to 0
95 percent confidence interval:
 -6763.929 19418.679
sample estimates:
mean in group High  mean in group Low 
          6546.500            219.125 
##########Whether high vs low infectivity groups differ in SERINC5 expression


### ANOVA : comparing low Vs medium Vs High
anova_model <- aov(Expression ~ Group, data = data)
summary(anova_model)
            Df   Sum Sq  Mean Sq F value   Pr(>F)    
Group        2 64094110 32047055   60.55 5.37e-07 ***
Residuals   12  6351298   529275                     
---
Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
###TukeyHSD post-hoc test
TukeyHSD(anova_model)
  Tukey multiple comparisons of means
    95% family-wise confidence level

Fit: aov(formula = Expression ~ Group, data = data)

$Group
                 diff         lwr       upr     p adj
Low-High    -6327.375 -7861.79506 -4792.955 0.0000004
Medium-High -5167.300 -6791.17755 -3543.422 0.0000057
Medium-Low   1160.075    53.58896  2266.561 0.0397990
###Variance test F test:
data_2 <- subset(data, Group %in% c("Low", "High"))

var.test(Expression ~ Group, data = data_2)

    F test to compare two variances

data:  Expression by Group
F = 30.959, num df = 1, denom df = 7, p-value = 0.001694
alternative hypothesis: true ratio of variances is not equal to 1
95 percent confidence interval:
     3.835098 29356.295624
sample estimates:
ratio of variances 
          30.95947 
###Normality check: Shapiro test
shapiro.test(data$Expression)

    Shapiro-Wilk normality test

data:  data$Expression
W = 0.68576, p-value = 0.0001757
############################################
## CONTINUATION: GENE-WISE CORRELATION
############################################

############################################
## -------- STEP 1: READ & MERGE EXPRESSION --------
############################################

# Reuse expr_folder already defined

files <- list.files(expr_folder, full.names = TRUE)

read_expr <- function(file) {
  df <- read.table(file, header = TRUE, sep = "\t", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  df <- df[, 1:2]

  srr <- tools::file_path_sans_ext(basename(file))
  cellline <- file_to_cellline[srr]

  colnames(df) <- c("GeneID", cellline)
  df
}

expr_list <- lapply(files, read_expr)

expr_merged <- Reduce(
  function(x, y) merge(x, y, by = "GeneID", all = TRUE),
  expr_list
)

############################################
## -------- STEP 2: ALIGN WITH INFECTIVITY --------
############################################

cell_order <- infect$CellLine

# Ensure all names exist
common_cells <- intersect(cell_order, colnames(expr_merged))

expr_mat <- expr_merged[, common_cells]

# Match infect order
infect_sub <- infect[infect$CellLine %in% common_cells, ]
expr_mat <- expr_mat[, infect_sub$CellLine]

expr_mat <- apply(expr_mat, 2, as.numeric)

############################################
## -------- STEP 3: GENE-WISE CORRELATION --------
############################################

cor_results <- apply(expr_mat, 1, function(gene_expr) {

  valid <- is.finite(gene_expr) & is.finite(infect_sub$Infectivity)

  if (sum(valid) < 3) {
    return(c(r = NA, R2 = NA, p = NA))
  }

  ct <- cor.test(
    gene_expr[valid],
    infect_sub$Infectivity[valid],
    method = "pearson"
  )

  c(
    r  = unname(ct$estimate),
    R2 = unname(ct$estimate)^2,
    p  = ct$p.value
  )
})

Warning in cor(x, y): the standard deviation is zero
############################################
## -------- STEP 4: RESULTS TABLE --------
############################################

cor_df <- data.frame(
  GeneID = expr_merged$GeneID,
  t(cor_results),
  row.names = NULL
)

cor_df$r  <- as.numeric(cor_df$r)
cor_df$R2 <- as.numeric(cor_df$R2)
cor_df$p  <- as.numeric(cor_df$p)

############################################
## -------- STEP 5: MULTIPLE TEST CORRECTION --------
############################################

cor_df$FDR <- p.adjust(cor_df$p, method = "BH")

############################################
## -------- STEP 6: SORT --------
############################################

cor_df <- cor_df[order(-abs(cor_df$r)), ]

############################################
## -------- OUTPUT --------
############################################

head(cor_df)
# Save results (optional)
write.csv(cor_df, "gene_correlation_results.csv", row.names = FALSE)

cor_df[cor_df$GeneID == "ENSG00000164300", ]
top_n <- 10
top_genes <- cor_df$GeneID[1:top_n]

# Extract expression of top genes
expr_top <- expr_merged[expr_merged$GeneID %in% top_genes, ]

# Remove GeneID column
expr_top_mat <- expr_top[, -1]

# Convert to numeric
expr_top_mat <- apply(expr_top_mat, 2, as.numeric)

# Log transform (important for visualization)
expr_top_mat <- log2(expr_top_mat + 1)

# Transpose for plotting
expr_top_t <- as.data.frame(t(expr_top_mat))

par(mar = c(6, 4, 2, 1))

boxplot(
  expr_top_t,
  las = 2,
  col = "grey85",
  border = "grey40",
  ylab = "Expression (log2)",
  main = "Top 10 Correlated Genes"
)

# Reorder rows to match top_genes order
expr_top <- expr_merged[match(top_genes, expr_merged$GeneID), ]

# Matrix
heat_mat <- expr_top[, -1]
heat_mat <- apply(heat_mat, 2, as.numeric)

# Log transform
heat_mat <- log2(heat_mat + 1)

# Set row names
rownames(heat_mat) <- top_genes

# Scale genes (important!)
heat_mat_scaled <- t(scale(t(heat_mat)))

# Heatmap
heatmap(
  heat_mat_scaled,
  Rowv = NA,
  Colv = NA,
  col = colorRampPalette(c("blue", "white", "red"))(50),
  margins = c(6, 6),
  main = "Top 10 Genes Heatmap"
)

############################################
## CLEAN VOLCANO PLOT (SMALL-n CORRELATIONS)
############################################

## thresholds
r_cutoff   <- 0.6
fdr_cutoff <- 0.05
top_n      <- 10   # number of genes to label

## prepare data
cor_df$negLogFDR <- -log10(cor_df$FDR)

cor_df$Group <- "Not significant"
cor_df$Group[cor_df$FDR < fdr_cutoff & cor_df$r >  r_cutoff] <- "Positive"
cor_df$Group[cor_df$FDR < fdr_cutoff & cor_df$r < -r_cutoff] <- "Negative"

cols <- c(
  "Positive"        = "firebrick3",
  "Negative"        = "steelblue",
  "Not significant" = "grey70"
)

## plot
par(mar = c(5, 5, 2, 1))

plot(
  cor_df$r,
  cor_df$negLogFDR,
  pch = 16,
  col = cols[cor_df$Group],
  cex = 0.7,
  xlab = "Pearson correlation (r)",
  ylab = expression(-log[10]("FDR")),
  main = "Gene expression vs Infectivity"
)

## threshold lines
abline(v = c(-r_cutoff, r_cutoff), lty = 2)
abline(h = -log10(fdr_cutoff), lty = 2)

## legend
legend(
  "topright",
  legend = names(cols),
  col = cols,
  pch = 16,
  bty = "n"
)

## label only top genes
top_hits <- cor_df[cor_df$Group != "Not significant", ]
top_hits <- top_hits[order(-abs(top_hits$r)), ][1:min(top_n, nrow(top_hits)), ]

if (nrow(top_hits) > 0) {
  text(
    top_hits$r,
    top_hits$negLogFDR,
    labels = top_hits$GeneID,
    pos = 3,
    cex = 0.7
  )
}

############################################
## CORRELATION vs MEAN EXPRESSION
############################################

cor_df$MeanExpr <- rowMeans(expr_mat, na.rm = TRUE)

plot(
  cor_df$r,
  log2(cor_df$MeanExpr + 1),
  pch = 16,
  col = cols[cor_df$Group],
  cex = 0.7,
  xlab = "Pearson correlation (r)",
  ylab = expression(log[2]("Mean expression (RPM + 1)")),
  main = "Correlation vs expression abundance"
)

abline(v = c(-r_cutoff, r_cutoff), lty = 2)

legend(
  "bottomright",
  legend = names(cols),
  col = cols,
  pch = 16,
  bty = "n"
)

correlation Vs expression-Abudance plot: Are strong correlations driven by low-expression noise or real biology? Many of these genes are moderately to highly expressed This suggests they are stable cellular programs, not noise

r_cutoff   <- 0.6
fdr_cutoff <- 0.05

high_corr_genes <- cor_df[
  cor_df$FDR < fdr_cutoff & abs(cor_df$r) > r_cutoff,
]

ensembl_ids <- unique(high_corr_genes$GeneID)
length(ensembl_ids)

NA
library(biomaRt)
Warning: package 'biomaRt' was built under R version 4.5.2
mart <- useMart(
  biomart = "ensembl",
  dataset = "hsapiens_gene_ensembl"
)

gene_annotation <- getBM(
  attributes = c(
    "ensembl_gene_id",
    "hgnc_symbol",
    "gene_biotype",
    "description"
  ),
  filters = "ensembl_gene_id",
  values = ensembl_ids,
  mart = mart
)

here the list gene_annotation contains names of the genes which are more correlated.

############################################
## BOXPLOT: TOP 10 GENES (GENE SYMBOLS + LOG SCALE)
############################################

## -------- SETTINGS --------
top_n <- 10

## -------- SELECT TOP GENES --------
top_genes <- cor_df[order(-abs(cor_df$r)), ][1:top_n, ]
genes_to_plot <- top_genes$GeneID

## -------- GET GENE SYMBOLS (ENSEMBL → HGNC) --------
library(biomaRt)

mart <- useMart("ensembl", dataset = "hsapiens_gene_ensembl")

gene_map <- getBM(
  attributes = c("ensembl_gene_id", "hgnc_symbol"),
  filters = "ensembl_gene_id",
  values = genes_to_plot,
  mart = mart
)

gene_labels <- gene_map$hgnc_symbol
names(gene_labels) <- gene_map$ensembl_gene_id

## fallback if symbol missing
gene_labels[gene_labels == "" | is.na(gene_labels)] <-
  names(gene_labels)[gene_labels == "" | is.na(gene_labels)]

## -------- PREP EXPRESSION MATRIX --------
cell_order <- infect$CellLine

expr_mat <- expr_merged[, cell_order]
expr_mat <- apply(expr_mat, 2, as.numeric)
rownames(expr_mat) <- expr_merged$GeneID

expr_sub <- expr_mat[genes_to_plot, , drop = FALSE]

## -------- LOG2 TRANSFORM --------
expr_list <- lapply(
  seq_len(nrow(expr_sub)),
  function(i) log2(expr_sub[i, ] + 1)
)

names(expr_list) <- gene_labels[genes_to_plot]

## -------- BOXPLOT --------
par(mar = c(8, 5, 2, 1))

boxplot(
  expr_list,
  outline = FALSE,
  las = 2,
  col = "grey85",
  border = "grey40",
  ylab = expression(log[2](RPM + 1)),
  main = "Top 10 genes most correlated with infectivity"
)

## -------- OVERLAY POINTS --------
for (i in seq_along(expr_list)) {
  points(
    jitter(rep(i, length(expr_list[[i]])), amount = 0.15),
    expr_list[[i]],
    pch = 16,
    col = "black"
  )
}
